title: API endpoint "network list" returns base64 encoded string instead of array of uint8 for field "Bytes"
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
Hi,
the [API reference for network list ](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html#operation/NetworkListLibpod) describes field `Bytes` as `Array of integers <uint8>`, boht for the initial result and the `Plugins` field. The API returns a base64 encoded string instead.


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. ``podman system service unix:///home/`whoami`/testing.sock --log-level=debug --time=500``

2. `podman network create testns`

3. ``` 
   curl --unix-socket /home/`whoami`/testing.sock "http://d/v3.0.0/libpod/networks/json"
   [{"Name":"testns","CNIVersion":"0.4.0","DisableCheck":false,"Plugins":[{"Network":{"type":"bridge","ipam":{"type":"host-local"},"dns":{}},"Bytes":"eyJicmlkZ2UiOiJjbmktcG9kbWFuMCIsImhhaXJwaW5Nb2RlIjp0cnVlLCJpcE1hc3EiOnRydWUsImlwYW0iOnsicmFuZ2VzIjpbW3siZ2F0ZXdheSI6IjEwLjg4LjIuMSIsInN1Ym5ldCI6IjEwLjg4LjIuMC8yNCJ9XV0sInJvdXRlcyI6W3siZHN0IjoiMC4wLjAuMC8wIn1dLCJ0eXBlIjoiaG9zdC1sb2NhbCJ9LCJpc0dhdGV3YXkiOnRydWUsInR5cGUiOiJicmlkZ2UifQ=="},{"Network":{"type":"portmap","capabilities":{"portMappings":true},"ipam":{},"dns":{}},"Bytes":"eyJjYXBhYmlsaXRpZXMiOnsicG9ydE1hcHBpbmdzIjp0cnVlfSwidHlwZSI6InBvcnRtYXAifQ=="},{"Network":{"type":"firewall","ipam":{},"dns":{}},"Bytes":"eyJiYWNrZW5kIjoiIiwidHlwZSI6ImZpcmV3YWxsIn0="},{"Network":{"type":"tuning","ipam":{},"dns":{}},"Bytes":"eyJ0eXBlIjoidHVuaW5nIn0="},{"Network":{"type":"dnsname","capabilities":{"aliases":true},"ipam":{},"dns":{}},"Bytes":"eyJjYXBhYmlsaXRpZXMiOnsiYWxpYXNlcyI6dHJ1ZX0sImRvbWFpbk5hbWUiOiJkbnMucG9kbWFuIiwidHlwZSI6ImRuc25hbWUifQ=="}],"Bytes":"","Labels":null}] ```


**Describe the results you received:**
A base64 encoded string.

**Describe the results you expected:**
An array of uint8.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.2.0
API Version:  3.2.0
Go Version:   go1.15.10
Git Commit:   0281ef262dd0ffae28b5fa5e4bdf545f93c08dc7-dirty
Built:        Tue Jan  1 01:00:00 1980
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +YAJL

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

Static version from: https://github.com/containers/podman/runs/2740486864

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Windows 10, 64bit, WLS2, Debian 10

comments.body: @arctic-alpaca Thanks for writing up this issue and your patience with this area of the API. A quick look at the code shows the Go json encoder is doing that "magic" base64 encoding for us.
